overall.description: A maritime simulation game depicting a ship's bridge at night, featuring three navigation monitors displaying charts and rotating radar. The vessel moves steadily through dark waters, with the bow visible through the forward windows and artificial lighting illuminating the control station.

events.description: distant navigation lights or shoreline illumination appears briefly on the starboard side

agents.ego_description: operating the vessel from the bridge, monitoring the rotating radar display and navigation charts

entered.detail: left screen displaying a colorful nautical chart with depth contours and navigation markers
center monitor showing a circular radar display with a rotating yellow sweep arm
right screen displaying ship telemetry data against a blue background
the forward deck and bow structure of the vessel visible through the bridge windows
dark water surrounding the ship's bow with subtle wave movement
metal safety railing on the port side of the bridge interior
metal safety railing on the starboard side of the bridge interior

segments.scene_description: The scene remains inside a ship's bridge throughout, with the camera fixed in a stationary position facing forward. Three navigation monitors dominate the upper view, with the center radar display continuously rotating its sweep arm. The vessel moves steadily through dark waters, and brief lights appear on the starboard side around the 15-second mark, suggesting passage near a harbor or navigation marker.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot maintains a steady, stationary perspective from within a ship's bridge as the vessel navigates through a dark maritime environment. It begins with a focus on three navigation monitors, where the center radar display continuously rotates its sweep arm against the backdrop of the night. As the ship moves steadily through the dark waters, the forward view remains constant, framing the bow through the bridge windows. The scene then shifts slightly in atmosphere as brief lights appear on the starboard side, suggesting the vessel is passing near a harbor or navigation marker before returning to the deep darkness of the open sea.
